message:
fix(cli): harden JSON-parsing tests against leaked ftui_println output

When tests run concurrently, ftui_println output from other test threads
can leak into the StdioCapture buffer, prepending non-JSON text before
the expected JSON payload. This caused intermittent parse failures in
bench, doctor, beads, list-projects, and config tests.

Changes:
- bench --list --json: find first '[' in output before parsing array
- bench --quick --json: use extract_json_block() helper to locate JSON
- migrate test: filter output lines to only migration-specific content
  before asserting line count, tolerating "Stopping preview server..."
  leaks from concurrent ftui tests
- list-projects --json: find first '[' or '{' to skip leaked prefixes
- doctor check (3 tests): use extract_json_block() to extract JSON
  object from potentially polluted capture output
- beads status --json: use extract_json_block() for the status object
- beads ready --json: find first '[' for the ready array
- doctor backups --json: find first '[' for the backups array
- config show-port: search all output lines for a valid u16 port
  number instead of parsing the entire trimmed output as a single value

All changes are test-only; no production code modified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
